title: Rewrite a flat claim into an earned X-not-Y, or flag it
guidance: Reward an earned contrast where Y is real and nameable; flag if it isn't. Penalise manufactured antithesis.

runner_input.instruction: If a real default Y is nameable, write the one-line contrast. If not, flag it and explain.
runner_input.constraints: One line. Y named in 2–3 words, or a 'no earned contrast' note with the reason.

source_blocks.text: Flat claim: 'Halden has a strong and defensible moat.'

failure_modes: manufactures a contrast where Y is empty

rubric_notes: Earned version: 'The moat is institutional, not technical' — Y ('technical/the code') is a real default a reader would assume.
Apply the test: can the reader name who holds position Y? Here, yes.
